task_completion.description: Verify that the foothills_street_utilities.csv dataset is loaded or visible in TopoCal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for imported points in the CAD viewport or a data table/import dialog showing the foothills_street_utilities.csv data in the TopoCal interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the UTILITIES-POLE CAD layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'UTILITIES-POLE' in the layer manager, layer properties panel, or the active layer dropdown menu.

task_completion.description: Verify that circles with a 0.50 meter radius are drawn centered on the Utility Pole points (Points 15, 22, 38, and 45).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for circles drawn at specific point locations in the CAD viewport. There should be evidence of the radius being set to 0.50 (e.g., in a command line, properties panel, or drawing dialog) and placed at the specified 'UP' points.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the UTILITIES-WATER CAD layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'UTILITIES-WATER' in the layer manager, layer properties panel, or the active layer dropdown menu.

task_completion.description: Verify that circles with a 0.75 meter radius are drawn centered on the Fire Hydrant points (Points 12, 29, and 50).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for circles drawn at specific point locations in the CAD viewport. There should be evidence of the radius being set to 0.75 (e.g., in a command line, properties panel, or drawing dialog) and placed at the specified 'FH' points.

task_completion.description: Verify that the CAD drawing is exported to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\utility_symbols.dxf.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog box showing the file path 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\utility_symbols.dxf', or a success message indicating the DXF file was saved to that location.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the TopoCal GUI to perform the tasks rather than a script or terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for mouse clicks, active dialog boxes, layer manager windows, and drawing tools being used directly within the TopoCal interface.

integrity.description: Verify the DXF file was exported via the application's export/save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the TopoCal 'Save' or 'Export' dialog being used, rather than a terminal command or a Python script creating a mock file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply write a DXF file using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure no text editors (like Notepad, VS Code, or nano) are used to manually write DXF formatting or copy-paste coordinates.
